content.problemStatement:
A (111-indexed) string SSS of length NNN is said to be **anti-palindrome** if, for each 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N, Si≠S(N+1−i)S_i \ne S_{(N + 1 - i)}Si​=S(N+1−i)​.

You are given a string SSS of length NNN (consisting of lowercase Latin letters only). Rearrange the string to convert it into an anti-palindrome or determine that there is no rearrangement which is an anti-palindrome.

If there are multiple rearrangements of the string which are anti-palindromic, print **any** of them.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains the string SSS.


Output Format
For each test case, if it is possible to rearrange SSS to convert it into an anti-palindrome, print `YES` in the first line and print the rearranged string in the second line. Otherwise, print `NO`.


You may print each character of `YES` and `NO` in uppercase or lowercase (for e.g. `yes`, `yEs`, `Yes` will be considered identical).


If there are multiple rearrangements of the string which are anti-palindromic, **print any of them**.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase Latin letters
It is guaranteed that the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.output:
NO
YES
abddcccc
YES
xxyy
NO
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: No matter how the string is rearranged, the condition Si≠S(N+1−i)S_i \ne S_{(N + 1 - i)}Si​=S(N+1−i)​ will not be satisfied for i=2i = 2i=2.
Test case 222: One possible rearrangement of the string which is anti-palindromic is abddcccc. Other answers, such as ccdaccdb, will also be accepted.
Test case 333: One possible rearrangement of the string which is anti-palindromic is xxyy.
Test case 444: It can be shown that none of the rearrangements of AAA is anti-palindromic.
